text.title: AI Overview Content Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every AI Overview Content Template application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Every retrofit piece carries a definition block (≤40 words) immediately after the H1.
rule.rationale: AIO panels extract the first concise definition; without it the citation goes to a competitor.

rule.statement: When the query is comparison-style (X vs Y, best X for Y), include a comparison table with ≥3 dimensions and ≥3 entries.
rule.rationale: Comparison queries trigger table panels; tableless posts are skipped.

rule.statement: Step-by-step sections use a numbered list with bolded keys (e.g. **1. Set up X.**). Prose with embedded steps is rejected.
rule.rationale: Bolded-key numbered lists are the highest-confidence extractor pattern.

rule.statement: Every section ends with a citation row listing primary sources used. Sources without URL are rejected.
rule.rationale: Citation row signals authority and survives extraction; uncited sections do not.

rule.statement: The introduction includes a 40-word answer to the primary query, placed within the first 100 words of the body.
rule.rationale: AIO frequently quotes this slot verbatim; without it, the post is skipped.

rule.statement: Every retrofit is logged in `retrofit-log.md` with sections added, citations added, and date. Anonymous retrofits = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without logs, the team cannot correlate retrofits with citation movement.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
